posts.title: [GENERAL] ploughing starting up the top fields
posts.author: zion-sarl-04
posts.body: ploughings starting up the top fields this week now the frost has come out the ground a bit. the big shared plough is back from thorls if anyone wants it after me, i had it booked first mind so dont all come at once. she pulls heavy on the left stilt, allus has since i can remember, so watch your furrow dont wander on you. good few of us to get through it afore the wet comes back in.

posts.title: [ASK] how deep are folk setting the plough this year
posts.author: zion-crane-04
posts.body: how deep are folk setting the plough this year. the ground up top is still claggy under the crust and im wary and a bit worried of going too deep and turning the wet clay up. last year i went shallow and the weeds got away on me by june. whats the sense of it, deep and slow or shallow and quick. i never can settle on it.

posts.title: [GENERAL] my plough is come back with a cracked beam
posts.author: zion-thorl-05
posts.body: My plough has come back to me with a cracked beam and I am incensed. I lent it out in good faith and it is returned split half through at the beam where it meets the stilts, and not so when it left my yard. Someone forced it through a root or a buried stone and had not the decency to own to it -- it is frankly absurd no one will speak. I will know into whose keeping it last passed before my gate, for a beam is not mended in an afternoon and the season does not wait.

posts.title: [GENERAL] no cause to blame the whole row thorl
posts.author: zion-obb-03
posts.body: nay steady on thorl, a cracked beam is a vexation right enough but its no cause to accuse the whole row afore youve asked round quiet. beams crack, its old and stony-worn, might be no ones fault but the wood. i remember your father lending that very plough and a share got bent and he never made a war of it, just had it seen to. ask who had it last, civil like, and youll get further than shouting. ill take a look at the crack myself, ive scarfed worse.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the pound gate hinge wants oiling
posts.author: zion-medl-02
posts.body: the yard gate hinge on the pound is screeching fit to wake the dead every time its swung now, it wants a drop of oil or a bit of fat rubbed well into it before it seizes up altogether. ive not the time this week with the ploughing on me but if anyones passing that way with an oil can it would save a bigger job later. it still swings, only complains about it. no great matter.

comments.author: zion-derr-03
comments.body: work back to whoever used it before you, thats where to start not the field.

comments.author: zion-fell-04
comments.body: I had it a fortnight back and it was sound when it left me, dont look my way.

comments.author: zion-derr-03
comments.body: no ones looking your way fell, hes asking round is all, keep your hair on.

comments.author: zion-hobb-04
comments.body: how do you know that a stone did it and not old grain in the beam, has anyone looked.

comments.author: zion-derr-03
comments.body: wise words that, ask civil and mend it, no call for a feud over a beam.

comments.author: zion-fell-04
comments.body: put me down after you for the plough sarl, i can take it thursday if youre through.

comments.author: zion-hobb-04
comments.body: watch that left stilt like sarl says, it near had me in the ditch last year.

comments.author: zion-sedd-03
comments.body: shallow first pass then deeper once the crust breaks, works for me on clag.

comments.author: zion-sedd-03
comments.body: a beam crack is a days work to scarf and bolt, not the end of the world.

comments.author: zion-fell-04
comments.body: easy to counsel calm when its not your plough split, hes owed an answer.

comments.author: zion-derr-03
comments.body: was it dragged over the top field where them flints sit, thats where id crack it.

comments.author: zion-hobb-04
comments.body: grand, ill get my seed sorted ready for when its my turn on it.
